column1: C[0xbdf: Can I leave messages on the board?
C[0xbdf: Sorry, ,could have SWORN I submitted that move. Took me a while to check back though..
C[0xbdf: So... How did you get into Go?
C[0xbdf: I was raised on chess by my dad. And while I think he always preferred Go, it was easier in american culture to have, for example, a Go club in elementary school which he ran.
0xbdf: He taught me Go though, though more briefly than Chess. I found it again though when I started at DigiPen.
C[cale.haskell: My Go story is much less interesting! I saw a thread on the something awful forums about Go - it linked to this tutorial: http://playgo.to/iwtg/en/ and I got a little bit hooked.
cale.haskell: I was a BSRTIS - currently a UI engineer at Bungie!
C[0xbdf: Seems like a good tutorial!

C[0xbdf: Do you know the site www.eidogo.com? It's always been a very good resource for me. It has a Joseki tutor, a database of professional games, lets you upload a .sgf to analyze, and a couple of other neat tricks.
cale.haskell: Ah, what's that link? That's neat.
C[egor.orlov.1988: Sorry. I don't know how i do this.
C[cale.haskell: doh, misclick on my phone. =[ Meant J8
C[saya: hi, good luck, have fun :)
C[egor.orlov.1988: Hello. You have one more stoun.
egor.orlov.1988:  Hello. You have one more stone.
